title: quercus garryana subsp. semota
intro: Quercus garryana subsp. semota, also known as the Oregon white oak, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to the Pacific coast of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Quercus garryana subsp. semota include supporting liver health, reducing inflammation, and acting as an antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
